title: Staphylococci: overview
body_html:
Staphylococci are a common type of bacteria which are often found normal commensal organisms but may also cause invasive disease. Some basic facts include:
  • Gram-positive cocci
  • facultative anaerobes
  • produce catalase

The two main types of Staphylococci you need to know about are Staphylococcus aureus and Staphylococcus epidermidis. These are summarised in the table below

Staphylococcus aureusStaphylococcus epidermidis
• Coagulase-positive
• Beta-haemolytic
• Causes skin infections (e.g. cellulitis), abscesses, osteomyelitis, toxic shock syndrome
• Coagulase-negative
• Non-haemolytic
• Cause of central line infections and infective endocarditis

Scanning electromicrograph of Staphylococcus aureus bacteria. Credit: NIAID
